nodes.label: Agent Orchestration
nodes.rationale: The coordinator dispatches domain work to specialists so role boundaries, handoffs, and reviewer gates remain enforceable.

nodes.label: Shared State Drop-Box Pattern
nodes.rationale: Per-agent inbox entries and Scribe-owned merging remove shared-file conflicts so independent agents can run concurrently.

nodes.label: Reviewer Rejection Protocol
nodes.rationale: A rejected artifact must be revised independently by another agent, preventing the original author from bypassing reviewer judgment.

nodes.label: Squad Source of Truth Hierarchy
nodes.rationale: Static governance and roster files remain authoritative while histories and logs are derived append-only evidence.

nodes.label: Autonomous Completion
nodes.rationale: The agent should retain control until every requested item is implemented and verified.

nodes.label: Adversarial Solution Validation
nodes.rationale: Red-team, stress, integration, and user-experience checks are used to expose failure modes before completion.

nodes.label: CommonMark Portability
nodes.rationale: Simple CommonMark constructs keep documentation readable in raw form, git diffs, terminals, and standard renderers.

nodes.label: Modular Azure Terraform Architecture
nodes.rationale: Resource-type modules promote reuse, consistent configuration, and clear separation of infrastructure concerns.

nodes.label: Managed Identity Authentication
nodes.rationale: Managed identities avoid stored credentials and support least-privilege Azure access.

nodes.label: App Configuration and Key Vault Pattern
Terraform Idempotency
nodes.rationale: Repeated applies should converge to zero changes, avoiding scripts and resources that recreate or conflict on every run.

nodes.label: Atomic Commits
nodes.rationale: One logical change per commit keeps history readable and supports automated changelog and CI workflows.

nodes.label: 95 Percent Confidence Gate
nodes.rationale: Clarification continues until requirements are sufficiently certain to avoid implementing an underspecified request.

nodes.label: Placeholder Self-Check
nodes.rationale: Generated artifacts must run without another developer filling in dummy values or unfinished blocks.

nodes.label: Canonical Hugo DOM Source of Truth
nodes.rationale: The deployed article DOM preserves the real rendered order, captions, links, and Hugo conversions more reliably than Markdown, RSS descriptions, or filenames.

nodes.label: Marker-Based Image Placement
nodes.rationale: Stable media markers preserve exact placement for opening images, adjacent figures, repeated prose, and flattened galleries where nearby-text anchoring is unreliable.

nodes.label: Explicit Publish Confirmation
nodes.rationale: Draft preparation is reversible but publishing is irreversible, so approval must be explicit and current.

nodes.label: Microsoft API Validation Workflow
nodes.rationale: Live official documentation and samples prevent hallucinated methods, wrong signatures, deprecated SDK patterns, and incorrect package names.

nodes.label: OIDC to UAMI to RBAC to Foundry Chain
Foundry Token Audience Fallback
nodes.rationale: The check retries with the Foundry audience after a Cognitive Services audience returns 401 because resource token requirements differ.

nodes.label: Single-Step Bearer Token Boundary
nodes.rationale: Token acquisition, inference, and assertion share one step so the bearer token never enters outputs, environment files, or logs.

nodes.label: Human-Reviewed Pull Request Gate
nodes.rationale: AI-generated text and images remain drafts until a person verifies technical rigor, voice, links, attribution, disclosure, and image rights.

nodes.label: Open PR Ledger Overlay
nodes.rationale: Ledger entries from open AI pull requests are overlaid before discovery so in-review topics participate in deduplication before reaching main.

nodes.label: Serialized Article Fan-Out
nodes.rationale: Candidate builds use max-parallel one to avoid races on branches and labels while fail-fast remains disabled so one bad candidate does not cancel the rest.

nodes.label: CODEOWNER Review Gate
nodes.rationale: Only an approved review by an identity listed in CODEOWNERS can promote an AI draft.

nodes.label: Approved PR Head Guard
nodes.rationale: Publication aborts if the PR head advances after approval, ensuring metadata updates apply only to the reviewed commit.

nodes.label: GitHub Issue Action Items
nodes.rationale: Retrospective actions become labeled GitHub issues because production data showed no completion for Markdown checklists and full completion after switching to issues.
